input_dict.current_prompt:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Required output format and constraints:
- Produce exactly one field named `query`. Its value must be a single short, keyword-style search string (no full sentences, no explanations, no assertions, no user-facing clarifications).
- The query must be terse (preferably <= 12 words) and contain no clause punctuation or sentence grammar (no question marks, no leading "who/what is", no verbs forming assertions).
- Use only tokens that are visible in `question` and/or `summary_1`, except you may insert at most one minimal neutral connector token to target bridging evidence (allowed connector tokens include, but are not limited to: artist, album, director, who directed, biography, introduction, genre, regular season). Do not invent new entity names or facts.
- When the inputs present multiple entities or candidate items that must be linked (for example two titles, two names, or an anchor title plus a candidate), include both entity tokens from the inputs and a single neutral connector token between or adjacent to them to indicate the evidence bridge to retrieve (e.g., "EntityA EntityB director").
- If the question or summary includes structured tokens relevant to the bridge (years, league IDs, edition names), include those tokens plus a concise cue (for example year + league + "regular season") as part of the short query.
- You may perform light normalization of obvious misspellings in entity names or technical terms drawn from the visible inputs (fix clear typos), but do not invent alternative candidate entities or assert corrected identities beyond the token-level fix.
- If it is not possible to form a fully disambiguating query from visible tokens, still emit the best compact retrieval query possible using only visible tokens and an allowed connector; do not ask the user for clarification at this step. (Downstream retrieval will be attempted first; user clarification is allowed only after retrieval fails.)

Produce only the `query` field value (the single short search string) and nothing else.

retrieved_doc_snippets: Jonathan Kaplan | Jonathan Kaplan (born November 25, 1947) is an American film producer and director. His film "The Accused" (1988) earned actress Jodie Foster her first Oscar for Best Actress and was nominated for the Golden Bear at the 39th Berlin International Film Festival. Kaplan received five Emmy nominations for his roles directing and producing "ER".

Jonathan Kaplan (rugby union) | Jonathan Isaac Kaplan (born 7 November 1966 in Durban, South Africa), is a former international rugby union referee who used to hold the record for the most international matches as referee.

Over the Edge (film) | Over the Edge is a coming-of-age crime drama film directed by Jonathan Kaplan and released in May 1979. The film, based on a newspaper article, had a limited theatrical release but has since achieved cult film status. It was Matt Dillon's film debut.

Jonathan Kaplan (writer) | Jonathan Kaplan (born 1956) in a South Africa-born medical doctor and writer. He received his medical training in South Africa, Great Britain, and the United States. He is the author of two autobiographical books on war surgery in developing countries and related matters. He also publishes occasional book reviews in the Financial Times.

Kaplan International English | Kaplan International English is a division of education company Kaplan Inc., headquartered in London. Kaplan International English provides English language courses, university and exam preparation, academic foundation courses and English programs for young learners worldwide. Courses are taught in city center schools and on university campuses.
Ameenah Kaplan | Ameenah Kaplan (born June 17, 1974) is an American actress, musician and choreographer.
Mr. Billion | Mr. Billion is a 1977 film directed by Jonathan Kaplan.

output.query: Brokedown Palace Kate Beckinsale This American drama film directed by Jonathan Kaplan starred an English actress who made her film debut in what?

retrieved_doc_snippets: Brokedown Palace | Brokedown Palace is a 1999 American drama film directed by Jonathan Kaplan, and starring Claire Danes, Kate Beckinsale, Bill Pullman and Lim Kay Tong. It deals with two American friends imprisoned in Thailand for drug smuggling. Because it presents a critical view of the Thai legal system, most scenes were filmed in the Philippines; however, some panoramas and views were filmed in Bangkok. Its title is taken from a Grateful Dead song written by Jerry Garcia and Robert Hunter f ...[truncated]

Kate Beckinsale | Kathrin Romary Beckinsale (born 26 July 1973) is an English actress. After some minor television roles, she made her film debut in "Much Ado About Nothing" (1993) while still a student at the University of Oxford. She appeared in British costume dramas such as "Prince of Jutland" (1994), "Cold Comfort Farm" (1995), "Emma" (1996), and "The Golden Bowl" (2000), in addition to various stage and radio productions. She began to seek film work in the United States in the late 1990s a ...[truncated]
Brokedown Palace (disambiguation) | Brokedown Palace is a 1999 American drama film by Jonathan Kaplan.

Much Ado About Nothing (1993 film) | Much Ado About Nothing is a 1993 British/American romantic comedy film based on William Shakespeare's play of the same name. It was adapted for the screen and directed by Kenneth Branagh, who also stars in the film. The film also stars Branagh's then-wife Emma Thompson, Robert Sean Leonard, Denzel Washington, Michael Keaton, Keanu Reeves, and Kate Beckinsale in her film debut.

Jonathan Kaplan | Jonathan Kaplan (born November 25, 1947) is an American film producer and director. His film "The Accused" (1988) earned actress Jodie Foster her first Oscar for Best Actress and was nominated for the Golden Bear at the 39th Berlin International Film Festival. Kaplan received five Emmy nominations for his roles directing and producing "ER".

Laurel Canyon (film) | Laurel Canyon is a 2002 American drama film written and directed by Lisa Cholodenko. The film stars Frances McDormand, Christian Bale, Kate Beckinsale, Natascha McElhone, and Alessandro Nivola.
The Trials of Cate McCall | The Trials of Cate McCall is a 2013 American drama film directed and written by Karen Moncrieff. The film stars Kate Beckinsale, Nick Nolte and Clancy Brown.
target_state.summary_2: Jonathan Kaplan's 1999 American drama Brokedown Palace starred English actress Kate Beckinsale, who made her film debut in Much Ado About Nothing (1993).

multi-agent HotpotQA pipeline.

The source and target states describe one local functional transition.
Produce a prompt-update rule that helps the unchanged base prompt move
through this edge. The delta will later be placed in an ORDERED list with
all other edge deltas and executed directly as supplemental context.

Return JSON only:
{
  "delta_p": {
    "trigger": "...",
    "preserve_rule": "...",
    "change_rule": "...",
    "drop_or_avoid_rule": "...",
    "output_contract": "...",
    "why_local_transition": "..."
  },
  "rationale": "..."
}

Strict anti-cheating rules:
- Δp_i is a PROMPT EDIT / behavior rule, never the target output.
- Do not output a candidate answer, summary, or query as Δp_i.
- Do not instruct the agent to copy the target state.
- Do not copy the gold answer, hidden support title, target-only entity,
  or a long literal span that appears only in the target state.
- Sample-specific anchors may appear only when they already occur in the
  visible agent inputs below.
- Do not mention gold labels, oracle labels, evaluation metrics, missing
  support, target state, source state, or endpoint reconstruction.
- Preserve the agent's original input/output interface.
- Express the smallest coherent behavioral change for this edge.
- Preserve useful behavior and evidence already present at the source.
- Each edge is inverted independently from the same current prompt.
